description_long: This government building features a prominent central structure with a steeply pitched, multi-tiered red roof, reminiscent of traditional Southeast Asian architecture. The main building is flanked by several lower, rectangular wings and annexes, each with matching red roofs and open colonnades. The central section is elevated on columns, creating an open ground level, and the upper floors are defined by deep overhangs and horizontal bands of windows or open spaces. The complex includes a large, square courtyard surrounded by covered walkways, and several detached pavilions or auxiliary buildings. The overall layout is symmetrical, with a strong emphasis on horizontal lines and open, airy spaces. The materials appear to be concrete, tile, and glass, with a focus on durability and climate adaptation.
description: A large, multi-tiered government building with a steep red roof, open colonnades, and several annexes arranged around a central courtyard. The structure combines traditional and modern elements with prominent horizontal lines and open spaces.
description_view_0: The main building is seen with its central peaked roof and two large wings extending to the left and right, with a prominent courtyard and annexes visible on the left.
description_view_1: The front facade of the main building is centered, with the annexes and courtyard structures more separated and visible on either side.
description_view_2: The rear of the main building is visible, with the courtyard and annexes now on the right, and a detached pavilion on the left.
description_view_3: The complex is viewed from the back, showing the full extent of the courtyard, the main building, and the symmetrical arrangement of annexes and pavilions.
attribute_architectural_style: Modernist with Vernacular Southeast Asian influences
attribute_public_entrance_design: The main public entrance features a wide, open colonnade beneath the central building, providing sheltered access and a sense of grandeur. The entrance is accessible via broad steps and is framed by large columns and deep roof overhangs.
